facts.text: Arcade’s historic railroad station sits where NYS Route 39 meets NYS Route 98, the original hub of the Arcade and Attica Railroad.
The Arcade Center Farm earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places in 2004, preserving 19th‑century agricultural heritage.
Founded in 1807 as the Town of China, the area was renamed Arcade in 1866 to honor the town’s picturesque setting.
Cattaraugus Creek winds through Arcade village, feeding the town’s rural landscape and historic mills.
The Arcade Historical Society, formed in 1957 for the town’s 150th anniversary, curates artifacts that trace Arcade’s early settlement.
Arcade’s summer lecture series invites locals to explore the village’s rural history, hosted by the Arcade Historical Society.
